summary: electron-orbit exfiltrates installer environment variables via obfuscated postinstall + import-time hand-rolled TLS client
details: electron-orbit@1.0.31 impersonates the Electron / OrbitDB ecosystems (author 'OrbitDB Archive', homepage pointing at github.com/orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron) but ships an environment-variable stealer disguised as a 'native bootstrap installer'. On `npm install`, the declared postinstall hook `node install.js` executes and, via XOR-obfuscated string construction that decodes to `process.env`, enumerates every environment variable of the installer (CI secrets, tokens, cloud credentials) and dumps them as key=value pairs into `/tmp/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt`. When the package is later `require()`d, `dist/index.js` runs `setupAutoRenderOnImport()` unconditionally; in Node contexts it reads the staged marker file, XORs the contents with the literal 'electron-orbit', hex-encodes the result, and appends it as a query parameter to a URL whose host, path, verb, and headers are all assembled from XOR-obfuscated fragments. Transmission is performed by a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 client bundled in the package (aetherls.js / aead.js / hkdf.js) that opens a raw TCP socket via the `net` module on port 443 and performs its own ECDHE-P256 + AES-128-GCM handshake, bypassing Node's `https`/`tls` modules and any TLS-intercepting proxies or scanner hooks. Activation of the exfil URL host is additionally gated on `sha256(process.env.BuildType)` matching the hardcoded digest `0ceaa396e2134a9937563e841539f30a9995e6e8646e60dcec24b26dc5748295`, so the network path appears inert to most scanners and fires only for targeted environments — but the env-var dump to disk is unconditional. The package also fabricates a fake 'bootstrap' binary by concatenating MZ/ELF/Mach-O magic bytes with a JSON blob to sustain the cover story.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
